ops.title: Plumb operating carrier through seat-advice targeting
ops.description: Review finding (2026-07-18, cc-present v2 rebuild): seat-advice targets dedupe and registry-classify on the MARKETING carrier (`flight_number[:2]` from seats.py normalization) — a Qatar-operated AA-numbered A350 classifies as AA (verdict `verify`) and different operators sharing marketing carrier + equipment + cabin collapse into one target. Mitigations in place: registry's honest `verify` fallback, and the researcher brief instructs checking the operating carrier on codeshares. Real fix: investigate whether the seats.aero trip-detail raw payload reliably carries an operating-carrier field, plumb it through `_normalize_segment` → `_leg` → `_seat_advice_targets` (dedupe + `quality.classify` on operating carrier, keep marketing for display), and thread it into `_thread_seat_advice`. Requires a store USER_VERSION bump.

ops.text: seats.aero raw payload investigated for a reliable operating-carrier field, finding recorded
if the field exists: operating carrier plumbed through normalization, targets, and fold, with quality.classify keyed on it and tests covering a codeshare segment
store USER_VERSION bumped alongside the normalization change
